headline: All seven majors below the 200-day line; funding is split, with shorts getting liquidated faster than longs, while whales move Bitcoin off exchange.
paragraphs: The regime is unambiguously bear. Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, XRP, BNB, Doge, and Cardano are all trading below their 200-day simple moving averages, with no golden crosses anywhere in the set. The distance from 12-month highs tells the story: Bitcoin sits 47.7% below its peak, Ethereum 60.6%, and Cardano the deepest at 82.5%. The market cap of 2.31 trillion is up 1.12% in 24 hours, but that is noise against the structural damage. Bitcoin dominance stands at 56.6%, a steady anchor, but it anchors a market that has given back months of gains.

Momentum shows ambiguity within the downtrend. Bitcoin's RSI-14 is at 57.1 and its MACD is above signal, suggesting the downward move itself may be resting rather than accelerating. Ethereum's RSI sits at 62.4, also above 50, with MACD positive. Five of seven assets have MACD above their signal line, yet none are above the 200-day moving average. This is a classic divergence: the daily momentum is not entirely spent, but the longer trend remains broken. Solana is the outlier with MACD below signal and RSI at 54.4, neither confirming strength nor confirming capitulation. Realized volatility is elevated across the board, with Ethereum and Solana at 46-49%, meaning swings are wide and uncertainty is baked in.

Derivatives positioning reveals a lopsided market with fragile long positioning. Bitcoin's long-to-short ratio on OKX is 1.26, reasonable, but Ethereum sits at 1.52, Solana at 2.41, and Doge at 4.7. When shorts get liquidated into strength, longs feel emboldened to lever up further. In the past 20 hours, Bitcoin shorts have liquidated at 9.5 million against only 4.7 million in long liquidations, a 2-to-1 ratio favoring short pain. Ethereum shows an even sharper pattern: 10.1 million in short liquidations versus 3.6 million in longs over 12 hours. Funding rates tell the other side. Bitcoin is charging 0.13% every 8 hours (annualized at 1.4%), which is low and not expensive; Ethereum is actually charging shorts, at negative 0.07% every 8 hours. But Solana, XRP, and Doge are all charging 0.47-1.0% per 8 hours, annualizing to 5-11%. These smaller caps are expensive to hold long, and that crowding is fragile.

Whale flows and dry powder show Bitcoin moving to cold storage while the float of stablecoins shrinks. In the past 48 hours, 133 million in Bitcoin has exited exchanges, with Kraken named as the venue. This is the behavior of holders moving coins to vaults, not urgency sellers. Over the past four weeks, whale flows have netted 1.58 billion, then 1.33 billion, then minus 110 million, then 276 million, a pattern of accumulation interrupted by a single week of outflow. Stablecoin float sits at 306.4 billion, down 2% in the past 30 days, meaning dry powder is being deployed or held back. The Fear and Greed index is at 29, labeled Fear, and has ranged from 11 to 29 over the past month, confirming the sentiment is depressed and volatile.

The disconnect worth naming is between momentum and regime. Daily signals suggest the selling pressure may be easing (RSI above 50 on four majors, MACD positive on five), yet all seven are still below the 200-day line and distances from 12-month highs are enormous. This is not yet a reversal pattern; it is a resting moment inside a downtrend. The liquidation data also matters: shorts are burning faster than longs, which can fuel a bounce, but the long positioning in smaller caps like Solana and Doge is expensive and crowded. ETF inflows remain positive (132.3 million into Bitcoin, 36.7 million into Ethereum as of July 17), a steady institutional bid that has accumulated to 51.4 billion and 11.1 billion cumulatively, yet the price action is still skeptical.

Watch for the 200-day moving average on Bitcoin and Ethereum. A reclaim above these lines would be a regime signal, not merely a bounce. Watch funding rates on Solana, XRP, and Doge: if those rates stay above 0.5% per 8 hours much longer, long crowding will become critical. Watch whale flows: another week of outflows after the recent pattern would signal accumulation is pausing. And watch the Fear and Greed index; a move back below 20 would confirm sentiment is not yet capitulative, while a move back above 50 would signal a shift in crowd psychology. Until one of these conditions moves, the tape reads as a resting seller in a confirmed downtrend, not a rally beginning.
